---
title: "RP 22: Reducing Stress"
format: html
---
# RP 22: Reducing Stress

Stress is one of the most common relapse pathways. Alcohol may seem like short-term relief, but it usually worsens stress over time through sleep disruption, relationship conflict, and reduced problem-solving capacity. Recovery improves when stress is addressed directly and early.

A practical model is: **reduce avoidable stress, tolerate unavoidable stress, and recover daily**.

Stress reduction is most effective when combined with stress recovery. Short daily recovery practices protect the nervous system, improve self-control, and reduce vulnerability to high-risk decision-making.

## Stress Plan
| Current stressor | Can I change it? (Y/N) | If yes: one action | If no: one coping skill |
|---|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |

::: {.callout-important}
### Practice plan

Work through this in real-life situations rather than only reading it.

Complete one "change action" and one "coping action" this week.

If something feels hard, simplify the step and repeat it consistently.
:::
